APPLICATION FOR OSC AND TRO; POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATIONS FILED BY PLAINTIFF AWADALLA, ABDULLAH

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF ABDULLAH AWADALLA'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: (OPPOSITION FILED UNTIMELY.) MOTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON HIS CLAIMS AND THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES AND HARMS WEIGHS IN PLAINTIFF'S FAVOR BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS ARE DESTROYING THE BUSINESS. DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM HANDLING THE RESTAURANT'S MONEY OR OTHERWISE INTERFERING IN THE OPERATION OF THE BUSINESS. DEFENDANT IS TO STAY 50 YARDS AWAY FROM THE RESTAURANT. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS A STAY AWAY ORDER REGARDING INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYEES, APPLICATION MUST BE MADE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 527.6 AND SET ON THE COURT'S HARASSMENT CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT THAT IS EQUAL TO DEFENDANT'S SALARY FOR ONE YEAR, THAT AMOUNT IS $20,800. PLAINTIFF TO POST BOND WITHIN TEN DAYS OF TODAY'S DATE. (PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL WILL PREPARE ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797
ORDER GRANTING ABDULLAH AWADALLA'S REQUEST FOR ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE AND PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION

Mtn To Recover Personal Items

MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CLAUDIUS OLIVIERA Motion to Recover Personal Items. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar. Motion not timely filed and served, per California Code of Civil Procedure 1005(b). (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Plaintiff Abdullah Awadalla'S Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-23-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Plaintiff Abdullah Awadalla's Order to Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction. Appearances: Philip Soderquist, Esq., Soderquist Law Offices, (415) 374-8500, appearing for Plaintiff and moving party; Defendant Claudius Oliviera, Defendant in Pro Per appearing and opposing. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: (Opposition filed untimely.) MOTION GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON HIS CLAIMS AND THE BALANCE OF EQUITIES AND HARMS WEIGHS IN PLAINTIFF'S FAVOR BECAUSE DEFENDANT'S ACTIONS ARE DESTROYING THE BUSINESS. DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM HANDLING THE RESTAURANT'S MONEY OR OTHERWISE INTERFERING IN THE OPERATION OF THE BUSINESS. DEFENDANT IS TO STAY 50 YARDS AWAY FROM THE RESTAURANT. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS A STAY AWAY ORDER REGARDING INDIVIDUAL EMPLOYEES, APPLICATION MUST BE MADE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 527.6 AND SET ON THE COURT'S HARASSMENT CALENDAR. PLAINTIFF TO POST A BOND IN THE AMOUNT THAT IS EQUAL TO DEFENDANT'S SALARY FOR ONE YEAR, that amount is $20,800., Plaintiff to post bond within ten days of today's date. (Plaintiff's counsel will prepare Order.)(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
